rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 8, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 2. DEFENDANT PETER KIEWIT SONS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to plaintiff¿s claims for strict liability, false representation and premises owner/contractor liability and denied as to negligence. Plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to respirable asbestos fibers as a result of defendant¿s activities. Plaintiff¿s hearsay objections to the two medical reports submitted by defendant are sustained. Even if considered, the medical reports do not negate any element of plaintiff¿s claims. Summary adjudication on plaintiff¿s claim for strict liability is granted because plaintiff concedes that defendant did not manufacture or supply asbestos-containing products and a contractor is not strictly liable for defective products unless it was a seller of those products. (Monte Vista Development Corp v. Superior Court (1991) 226 Cal. App. 3d 1681) Summary adjudication on plaintiff¿s claim for false representation is granted because plaintiff does not dispute that he cannot prove this claim and, while he represents that the claim has been dismissed, no dismissal has been filed. Summary adjudication on plaintiff¿s claim for premises owner/contractor liability is granted because it is undisputed that plaintiff does not posses evidence that defendant was the owner of plaintiff¿s worksite or that he was exposed to asbestos through the activities of a contractor hired by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY SAAVEDRA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 8, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 3. DEFENDANT MOORE BROS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony creates a triable fact whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing blankets supplied by Moore Bros. Defendant¿s D¿Amico objection is overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WELTON OLIVER JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 8, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 5. DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Calaveras¿ alternative motion for summary adjudication is moot as to false representation (issue 1), premises owner/contractor liability (issue 2), and punitive damages (issue 3), as plaintiff has withdrawn these claims; and denied as to exposure to asbestos attributable to Calaveras (issue 4). Per California Rule of Court 3.13150(b), a moving party may not seek summary adjudication as to a certain set of facts not rising to a complete cause of action. Plaintiff presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attributable to Calaveras while working with transite pipe at Westland Water District. Calaveras¿ objections to the Johns-Manville invoices and Harold Kahlen¿s trial testimony in Cookus v. Calaveras are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD VOGEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 8, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 9. Defendant Mannington Mills, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration and defendant¿s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to defendant¿s asbestos-containing vinyl sheet flooring. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT SANDERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 8, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 10. Defendant Bayer Cropscience¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the claims for false representation and punitive damages and denied as to the claims for negligence and strict liability. Plaintiff¿s declaration and defendant¿s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to respirable asbestos fibers from products manufactured by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s request to continue the motion for an opportunity to re-depose plaintiff is denied. Mr. Sage¿s declaration that any asbestos in defendant¿s products would have been encapsulated and bound by resins, asphalts and plasticizers is too vague and uncertain in its application to the facts of this case to negate the element of any claim. As to the claims for false representation and punitive damages, defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting plaintiff¿s factually devoid discovery responses and plaintiff failed to create a triable fact. While plaintiff represents that these claims have been dismissed, no dismissal has been filed. = (220/HEK)
